sources/ugc-net-cs-2021-nov-with-answers.reason: The question tests propositional logical equivalence.
The question tests floor-function identities in discrete mathematics.
The question is a constrained counting/surjection problem.
The question is a direct application of the pigeonhole principle.
The staircase problem is a discrete counting recurrence.
Wheel-graph regularity is a graph-theory question.
The question tests graph planarity and Kuratowski subdivisions.
The stem concerns semigroup identities and rectangular bands.
The question asks for the language generated by a context-free grammar.
The stem asks which formal languages are nonregular.
Depth-first order, dominators and reducible CFGs are compiler loop-analysis topics.
The stem asks for the result of a C function; binary representation appears only inside the program logic and options.
The stem is a Java recursive-function trace, not computer arithmetic or architecture.
JNDI, RMI, JDK and JRE are Java programming-platform concepts.
Imperative, object-oriented, logic and functional paradigms belong to programming-language concepts.
Image bit depth and raster storage are computer-graphics representation topics.
The question asks for a 2-D affine transformation matrix in computer graphics.
The question tests cubic Bezier-curve properties in computer graphics.
MVC component responsibilities are a software architectural-design topic.
Availability, MTBF and repair time are software reliability and quality metrics.
Source-control repository contents are part of Software Configuration Management.
